ideation: This sequence simulates conducting an orchestra. The right arm will perform the sweeping motions of a conductor, while the left arm remains mostly still, providing a counterpoint to the dynamic movement.  I'll use the two joints to create expressive, yet fluid, movements. The sequence will begin with a neutral position, transition to an upward sweep, then a downward sweep, before returning to the neutral position.  In the final frame, there will be a slight pause before the next conducting movement. I'll leverage the provided examples to ensure the movements are natural and the transitions are smooth.

frames.beat: arms rest, ready to lead the music
right arm sweeps up, cueing the orchestra
right arm drives down, marking the beat's rhythm
arms return to center, a moment of reflection
